definitions.text: The command to make ready, regardless of language of competitors, used in multiple sports to get contestants to their marks in preparation to start.
Prepared for immediate action or use.
examples: I made ready to set out on my journey.
The troops are ready for battle.

definitions.text: first only used predicatively, freely used from the end of the 17th century .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: The cave was like a ready-made home for us.

definitions.text: To prepare; to make ready for action.
